epigraph.quote: The curtain does not end the performance. The Griot who carried the truth in the firelight carried it in the village too. That is what the community trusted. Not the performance. The person.

blocks.text: The actor who is magnificent on stage and dismissive in the green room has not understood what performance is.
They think the performance happens in the lights. The community knows it happens in everything.
Etiquette is not a list of rules.
The daily, lived expression of the prior contract. In every room. On every set. In every public encounter. Online. In private. When no one is watching and especially when everyone is.
Thirty-two people. Every one in service of the same story.
A late actor is not making a personal choice about their morning. They are making a professional choice about thirty-two people's time.
The actor who treats crew as invisible has not understood what a set is. The crew knows the difference between performed warmth and genuine respect. They remember. The industry is a village.
Does this serve the truth or only the moment's appetite?
Write it. Wait. Ask again. If the answer is still only yourself — delete it. The public record is permanent. The feeling passes.
You are building the market. Every day. Whether you intend to or not. The African actor who builds a reputation for integrity is not just building their own career.
They are building the market for the actor who comes after them.
Acting is not a hobby or a talent. It is a responsibility. To the craft. To the audience. To the market you are building. To the actor who will inherit that market.
That responsibility does not begin when the camera rolls. It does not pause when it stops. It is the way of being that the prior contract requires. Always.

requirement.prompt: Post one specific commitment you are making to how you carry the craft outside of performances. Not a vague intention — a specific, measurable, public commitment. On record. Include #JoelhoodP4L5.
